Looking at the image, the platform has moved RIGHT twice and is now near the opening at the top. The platform is at rows 25-29, cols 34-38 (approximately). The target is at rows 11-13, cols 35-37.
Now I need to move UP to reach the target's row. Let me try moving UP to see if the platform can reach the opening.
But wait, I need to check if the platform can move UP from its current position. The opening is at rows 8-16, cols 32-40. The platform is at rows 25-29, cols 34-38. If I move UP, the platform would move to rows 20-24, which is still below the opening. If I move UP again, the platform would move to rows 15-19, which overlaps with the opening (rows 8-16).
Let me try moving UP twice to see if the platform can reach the opening.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:

<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current platform position
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] in 'Ob':
        print(f"ID {n['id']}: color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
